policy_option: Conservative status quo: keep all current RISK transcriptomic and methylation rows as same-family but countability Unclear
evidence_supporting_option: All four rows are public RISK-family pediatric IBD/Crohn resources. DS005 exposes CCFA_Risk identifiers; DS040 exposes rectal accession-local/numeric IDs and has confirmed public ID-stem overlap with DS121 for at least 35 stems; DS041 exposes generic Sample1-Sample245 labels; DS121 exposes visit-style blood methylation IDs. Public metadata do not reconcile all four into one subject crosswalk.
risks: Users cannot get a de-duplicated RISK subject count directly from the registry; cohort counting remains conservative and may under-support automated summaries.
recommended_current_action: Recommended now. Keep component_of_dataset_id blank for DS005, DS040, DS041, and DS121. Keep count_as_independent_cohort=Unclear for all four. Use overlap_notes to warn not to sum RISK rows without subject-level reconciliation.
evidence_required_before_converting_uncl: A source-controlled RISK subject map linking CCFA_Risk IDs, DS040 rectal IDs, DS041 Sample IDs, and DS121 blood methylation visit IDs; confirmation of whether controls and IBD cases are shared or distinct across accessions; visit/timepoint mapping; rule for whether a future parent row exists.
recommended_field_values_if_adopted: study_family stays RISK_pediatric_IBD_mucosal_RNAseq for DS005/DS040/DS041 and RISK_pediatric_Crohn_methylation for DS121 unless a broader parent-family field is added; component_of_dataset_id blank; count_as_independent_cohort Unclear.
uncertainty_notes: This option explicitly treats absence of a public crosswalk as uncertainty, not independence.

policy_option: Confirmed-pair caution: mark DS040 and DS121 as overlapping in notes but do not assign parent/component
evidence_supporting_option: OQ135 public matrix comparison found at least 35 shared ID stems between DS040 rectal RNA-seq and DS121 blood methylation after stripping DS040 rectal suffixes and DS121 visit suffixes. They are different tissues and omics layers, so neither is a clean component of the other.
risks: If users see both rows as Unclear they may still accidentally sum them; if a parent is forced, the registry may imply a hierarchy that public evidence does not support.
recommended_current_action: Use as part of current notes. Keep both count_as_independent_cohort=Unclear and component_of_dataset_id blank. State that DS040 and DS121 should not be counted as independent RISK cohorts.
evidence_required_before_converting_uncl: Full subject-level crosswalk and visit map; confirmation whether DS121 includes only a subset of DS040 or additional RISK subjects; confirmation whether blood methylation controls overlap rectal transcriptomics controls.
recommended_field_values_if_adopted: No component assignment now. If full source map later proves DS121 is a subset/component of a selected RISK parent row, set component_of_dataset_id to that parent and count_as_independent_cohort=FALSE; do not set DS121 component_of_dataset_id=DS040 solely from current evidence.
uncertainty_notes: Confirmed pairwise overlap is real but incomplete; it does not resolve DS005 or DS041.

policy_option: Future parent-row model: create or designate a RISK parent counting unit and make modality/site accessions components
evidence_supporting_option: All four rows are from the RISK pediatric IBD/Crohn ecosystem and include different sites or omics layers: ileal RNA-seq, rectal RNA-seq, ileal Crohn RNA-seq, and blood methylation. A parent model would make cohort counting easier if a verified source-level subject map exists.
risks: Premature parent assignment could hide true independent subcohorts, mix CD-only and broader IBD/control rows, or incorrectly collapse distinct sampling sites/timepoints. It may also require a non-accession parent row or selection of an arbitrary accession as parent.
recommended_current_action: Do not implement now. Keep as future option only.
evidence_required_before_converting_uncl: Source-controlled subject map across all four rows; confirmed RISK cohort definitions; disease/control group harmonization; tissue and visit/timepoint mapping; decision whether parent row is an existing dataset_id or a new non-primary counting artifact.
recommended_field_values_if_adopted: If later adopted, one verified parent would carry count_as_independent_cohort=TRUE and component rows would carry component_of_dataset_id=<parent> and count_as_independent_cohort=FALSE. Parent choice should be documented in a separate patch plan before registry edits.
uncertainty_notes: Public evidence currently supports family grouping, not a complete parent/component hierarchy.

policy_option: Do not use sample counts as independent-cohort evidence
evidence_supporting_option: Current rows have different sample counts and tissues: DS005 has 322 public ileal RNA-seq samples in the series, DS040 has 190 rectal RNA-seq samples, DS041 has 245 ileal samples, and DS121 has 402 blood methylation samples. Differences are expected from tissue, omics, visit, and inclusion criteria, not necessarily independent subjects.
risks: Using sample counts as independence evidence would overcount multi-tissue or multi-omic RISK participants; using count differences to infer non-overlap is unsafe.
recommended_current_action: Apply immediately as counting guidance. Any automated cohort summary should exclude or separately flag RISK rows with count_as_independent_cohort=Unclear.
evidence_required_before_converting_uncl: Subject-level mapping, not sample-count comparison. Required fields should identify individual participant, visit/timepoint, tissue, assay, diagnosis/control status, and whether repeated measures are present.
recommended_field_values_if_adopted: No direct field changes beyond retaining Unclear and explicit overlap_notes.
uncertainty_notes: This policy is especially important because DS121 is cross-omics and longitudinal/visit-labeled.

policy_option: Manual-review conversion rule for TRUE/FALSE/component values
evidence_supporting_option: Existing evidence is asymmetric: DS040-DS121 overlap is confirmed, but DS005 and DS041 cannot be publicly crosswalked to the others from GEO metadata alone. Therefore field conversion requires stronger evidence than shared RISK naming.
risks: Without a strict conversion rule, future patches could convert one row to TRUE or FALSE based on incomplete pairwise evidence and create misleading downstream counts.
recommended_current_action: Before any conversion from Unclear, produce a row-level patch plan that cites the exact source map and states the counting unit. Do not convert a single RISK row in isolation.
evidence_required_before_converting_uncl: For TRUE: proof that a row's subjects are independent of all other current RISK rows or that it is the selected verified parent counting row. For FALSE/component: proof that the row's subjects are fully contained in a specified parent counting row, or a documented rule for partial overlap. For component_of_dataset_id: clear parent/child relation, not just same family or partial overlap.
recommended_field_values_if_adopted: Keep current RISK rows as Unclear until the conversion evidence is available. If partial overlap only, preserve Unclear and explain partial overlap in overlap_notes rather than forcing TRUE/FALSE.
uncertainty_notes: Recommended rule preserves uncertainty and prevents overcounting until full RISK subject reconciliation is available.
